title: Arenal Volcano Hike
shortDescription: Trek across hardened lava fields and rainforest at the base of Costa Rica's most iconic active volcano with a bilingual naturalist guide.
meetingPoint: Hotel pickup in La Fortuna area

image.alt: Arenal Volcano rising above tropical rainforest with clouds at the summit, Costa Rica

fullDescription: Trek across hardened lava fields and through tropical rainforest at the base of Costa Rica's most iconic active volcano. Guests explore Arenal National Park's well-groomed trails under the shadow of the 1,670-meter cone while a bilingual naturalist guide reveals the forest's secrets — from rare tree frogs to howler monkeys, giant ferns to 400-year-old ceiba trees. The morning option loops around pristine rainforest with views over Lake Arenal; the afternoon option follows the historic Arenal 1968 lava flow trail created during the volcano's catastrophic eruption.
highlights: Hike across the 1968 lava fields on the Bosque 1968 loop (4.7 km) or Lava Flow Trail (2.5 km)
Spot wildlife: howler monkeys, tree frogs, iguanas, toucans, and scarlet macaws
Identify native plants: Pacaya palms, giant ferns, heliconia, and a 400-year-old ceiba tree
Panoramic views of Lake Arenal and the volcano cone
Expert bilingual naturalist guide throughout
Fruit snack and water provided on trail
Suitable for families and all fitness levels
itinerary: Hotel pickup in La Fortuna zone (7:30 AM or 1:30 PM departure)
25-minute shuttle to Arenal National Park entrance
Naturalist guide briefing and trail selection
Morning option: Rainforest loop — bird watching, wildlife spotting, Lake Arenal overlook
Afternoon option: Arenal 1968 Trail — lava fields, Bosque 1968 loop, volcano overlook point
Mid-trail fruit snack and water break
Return shuttle to La Fortuna (~12:30 PM or 4:30 PM arrival)
notIncluded: Personal gear (rain jacket, bug spray, closed-toe shoes)

title: Manuel Antonio Wildlife Walk
shortDescription: Explore one of the world's most biodiverse national parks on a guided walk through old-growth rainforest and pristine Pacific beaches.
location: Manuel Antonio National Park, Pacific Coast
meetingPoint: Park entrance or hotel pickup from Quepos and Manuel Antonio hotels

image.alt: Aerial view of Manuel Antonio National Park's lush rainforest coastline meeting turquoise Pacific waters

fullDescription: Explore one of the world's most biodiverse national parks on a guided wildlife walk through old-growth rainforest, pristine white-sand beaches, and rich coastal habitats. Manuel Antonio is the only Costa Rican park where you can spot three of the country's four tropical primate species in a single morning. A bilingual naturalist guide leads you along the flat 2-mile trail, using spotting scopes to reveal camouflaged sloths, dart frogs, and canopy birds that most visitors never notice. The tour ends with a 20-minute leisure stop at the park's stunning Third Beach.
highlights: Three primate species in one park: white-faced capuchins, howler monkeys, and squirrel monkeys
Expert bilingual naturalist guide with spotting scope for canopy wildlife
Sloths, iguanas, coatis, poison dart frogs, and 350+ bird species
Lush rainforest canopy trails merging with turquoise Pacific coastline
20-minute leisure beach stop at Third Beach
Small group size for personalized wildlife encounters
Transportation from Quepos and Manuel Antonio hotels included
itinerary: Hotel pickup from Quepos or Manuel Antonio (7:50 AM or 12:50 PM)
Arrive at Manuel Antonio National Park entrance; guide check-in
Naturalist briefing on park ecology and trail rules
Enter park on flat loop trail — rainforest section with wildlife spotting (sloths, monkeys, birds)
Mid-trail reptile and amphibian observation (iguanas, frogs)
Beach stop at Third Beach (20 minutes)
Return along coastal trail
Exit park; transportation back to hotel (~11:30 AM or 4:30 PM)
included: Round-trip transportation from Quepos and Manuel Antonio

title: Monteverde Cloud Forest Guided Hike
shortDescription: Walk through one of Earth's most ecologically rich ecosystems — a cloud forest on the Continental Divide where mist, orchids, and quetzals converge.
location: Monteverde Cloud Forest Reserve, Puntarenas Province

image.alt: Visitors walking on hanging bridges through the Monteverde cloud forest canopy, Puntarenas, Costa Rica

fullDescription: Walk through one of Earth's most ecologically rich ecosystems — a cloud forest perched on the Continental Divide at 1,440 meters altitude where Caribbean mist collides with Pacific air. The Monteverde Cloud Forest Reserve protects over 10,500 hectares of primary and secondary forest harboring 500+ bird species, 100+ reptile species, 755 tree species draped in bromeliads and orchids, and one of the last refuges for the endangered resplendent quetzal. Trail circuits range from 0.9 to 2.5 miles. Limited to 26 visitors per time slot; pre-booking required.
highlights: Chance to spot the iconic resplendent quetzal (best March–April)
500+ orchid species — highest density in the world
Hanging bridge with canopy-level bird-watching using spotting scope
Waterfall visit included on selected trail circuits
Hummingbird garden observation
Expert bilingual naturalist guide in English or Spanish
Conservation history dating to the 1970s Quaker-led land rescue
itinerary: Hotel pickup in Monteverde
Drive to Reserve entrance (10–15 min)
Naturalist guide briefing; select trail circuit: Essence (0.9 mi), Heart of Forest (1.7 mi), or Continental Divide (2.5 mi)
Guided hike — rainforest layers, bird ID with scope, orchid and bromeliad observation
Hanging bridge crossing (Heart of Forest and Continental Divide circuits)
Waterfall stop (Essence and Continental Divide circuits)
Hummingbird garden visit
Return transportation to Monteverde lodging
included: Park entrance fee ($29 USD per circuit)
notIncluded: Personal gear (rain jacket, rubber boots recommended)

title: Caribbean Coast Snorkel — Cahuita National Park
shortDescription: Discover Costa Rica's largest coral reef and lush coastal rainforest on a combined snorkeling and wildlife hike along the Caribbean shore.
location: Cahuita National Park, Puerto Viejo, Caribbean Coast
meetingPoint: Puerto Viejo area office or hotel pickup from Manzanillo area

image.alt: Aerial view of a white-sand beach surrounded by tropical trees near Puerto Viejo, Caribbean Coast of Costa Rica

fullDescription: Discover the Caribbean's crown jewel: Cahuita National Park protects Costa Rica's largest coral reef system, covering 600 hectares of living reef and 35,000 hectares of coastal rainforest. After a scenic 20-minute boat ride, plunge into warm crystalline waters for two snorkeling sessions among 6 coral species and 123 fish species. Between sessions, rest on the white-sand beach at Punta Cahuita with tropical fruits before a guided 1-hour rainforest hike where monkeys and sloths perform above the trail.
highlights: Costa Rica's largest coral reef — 6 coral species, 123 fish species
Two snorkeling sessions in warm Caribbean waters
Potential encounters with sea turtles, reef sharks, and spotted eagle rays
Fruit and snack break on pristine Punta Cahuita beach
1-hour guided rainforest hike: howler monkeys, sloths, poison dart frogs
All snorkeling gear and insurance included
Small groups for optimal wildlife and reef experience
itinerary: 7:00 AM — Pickup from hotels in Manzanillo, Puerto Viejo, or Cahuita area
7:30 AM — Depart to Cahuita; guide commentary on Caribbean culture and ecology
8:00 AM — Arrive at park entrance; safety briefing and gear fitting
8:30 AM — Board boat for 20-minute ride; first snorkeling session (coral gardens, tropical fish)
9:00 AM — Second snorkeling session (different reef section)
9:30 AM — Beach stop at Punta Cahuita; fruit and snacks break
10:00 AM — Begin 1-hour guided rainforest hike (coastal forest, wildlife)
11:00 AM — Return to vehicle; depart
12:00 PM — Arrive back at Puerto Viejo

title: Coffee Farm Tour — Central Valley
shortDescription: Follow the bean-to-cup journey on a working certified estate in the volcanic highlands where some of the world's finest Arabica coffee grows.
priceNote: Half-day $35–$65; Full-day $65–$100. Recommended operators: Doka Estate, Café Britt, Aquiares, Finca Rosa Blanca.
meetingPoint: Hotel pickup from San José or airport area

image.alt: Woman picking ripe coffee cherries from a tree at a Starbucks coffee farm in Costa Rica's Central Valley

fullDescription: Costa Rica's Central Valley is the birthplace of one of the world's most prized coffees — Arabica beans grown at 1,200–1,700 meters altitude in volcanic soil under shade trees. This hands-on tour takes you through the entire bean-to-cup journey on a working certified estate. Walk through rows of coffee plants, pick ripe cherries during harvest season (October–February), and follow the process through wet mill, drying beds, hulling, roasting, and professional cupping.
highlights: Hands-on coffee cherry picking (especially October–February harvest season)
Full seed-to-cup process: wet mill, sun-drying, hulling, roasting
Professional coffee cupping session with aroma and flavor analysis
140+ bird species on-farm
Visit to traditional sugar cane mill (Doka Estate)
Butterfly garden and orchid collection (Doka Estate)
Sustainable and Rainforest Alliance-certified farming practices
itinerary: 7:30–8:00 AM — Hotel pickup from San José or airport area
8:30 AM — Welcome coffee and pastries at farmhouse
9:00 AM — Guided walk through coffee plantation: plant life cycle, shade trees, soil
10:00 AM — Cherry picking experience (seasonal) or demonstration
10:30 AM — Wet processing mill tour: pulping, fermentation, washing
11:00 AM — Drying beds and sun-drying methods
11:30 AM — Roasting facility tour
12:00 PM — Professional cupping session: aroma wheel, flavor profiles, brewing methods
12:30 PM — Farm shop; return transport to San José
included: Round-trip transportation from San José or airport

title: Tortuguero Sea Turtle Night Tour
shortDescription: Witness nesting Green Sea Turtles on the black-sand beaches of Tortuguero — one of the most important nesting sites in the Western Hemisphere.
priceNote: Plus $10–$20 optional boat transfer. Park entrance fee paid separately.

seasonality.note: Green Sea Turtle nesting peak season. Tour does not operate outside these dates.

meetingPoint: Tortuguero village; hotel pickup via boat available for outlying lodges

image.alt: Green sea turtle hatchling making its way across the sand toward the ocean at night

fullDescription: Tortuguero — 'Land of Turtles' — is where the largest nesting population of Atlantic Green Sea Turtles returns every year to lay eggs on the same black-sand beaches where they were born. This intimate night tour, led by SINAC-certified naturalist guides, takes groups of maximum 15 people to witness one of nature's most primal spectacles: a 150-kilogram turtle hauling herself from the surf, excavating a nest, and depositing up to 120 eggs. A 90% chance of turtle sightings during peak season. Camera use is prohibited to protect the turtles.
highlights: Witness Green Sea Turtles nesting or hatchlings emerging (July–October)
Maximum 15 guests per group for an intimate, respectful experience
SINAC-certified guides with expert knowledge of turtle behavior
90% turtle sighting rate during peak season
One of the most important nesting sites in the Western Hemisphere
Complements daytime canal boat tours (separate activity)
No cameras or flash allowed — a fully immersive nature encounter
itinerary: Afternoon — Time slot assigned by daily draw (8 PM or 10 PM)
7:30 / 9:30 PM — Meet naturalist guide at designated meeting point in Tortuguero village
Guide briefing: rules, behavior protocols, turtle biology
8:00 / 10:00 PM — Silent beach walk to turtle monitoring section
Observe nesting female: excavating, egg-laying, covering nest, returning to sea (or hatchling emergence)
Guide provides ecological commentary in whispered English or Spanish
~11:00 PM / 1:00 AM — Return to village or hotel
notIncluded: Boat transfer to or from outlying lodges ($10–$20 per person)
Transportation to Tortuguero (accessible only by boat or small plane)

title: Pacuare River White-Water Rafting
shortDescription: Raft 30 km of Class III–IV rapids through a pristine rainforest canyon on one of National Geographic's top 5 rivers in the world.
location: Río Pacuare, Siquirres, Cartago / Limón border
duration: Full day (~11.5 hours; 3 hours on river)
meetingPoint: Hotel pickups from San José (6:00 AM), Caribbean Coast, or Arenal / La Fortuna

image.alt: Group of people rafting through white-water rapids on a river surrounded by dense rainforest in Costa Rica

fullDescription: Named one of the world's top 5 rivers to raft by National Geographic, the Río Pacuare carves through 30 kilometers of pristine primary rainforest canyon accessible only by river. Class III–IV rapids alternate with calm jungle pools where sloths dangle from riverside trees and toucans streak overhead. The 38-rapid descent drops through a dramatic basalt gorge flanked by 100-foot waterfalls, including the spectacular 150-foot Huacas waterfall. Professional bilingual guides provide constant coaching and safety throughout.
highlights: 38 rapids over 30 km on one of the world's premier white-water rivers (National Geographic Top 5)
Class III–IV rapids through a pristine primary rainforest canyon
150-foot Huacas waterfall and dramatic Pacuare Gorge
Sloth, toucan, parrot, and frog sightings from the raft
Professional bilingual certified guides; full safety gear included
Breakfast and traditional Costa Rican lunch included
Pickups from San José, Caribbean Coast, or Arenal
itinerary: 6:00–6:30 AM — Hotel pickups (San José / Caribbean / Arenal)
8:15 AM — Arrive at Exploration Center; hearty breakfast
9:00–9:30 AM — Transfer to river access point at Bajo Pacuare
9:30–10:15 AM — Safety orientation and paddle technique practice on calm water
10:15 AM — Begin river descent: Class I–II warm-up section
11:00 AM — Enter Pacuare Gorge; Class III–IV rapids begin
11:30 AM — Huacas waterfall stop and swim break
1:15 PM — Reach take-out point; board transport
1:30 PM — Return to Exploration Center; hot showers
2:00 PM — Traditional Costa Rican lunch
2:30 PM — Depart; arrive back at origin ~5:00 PM
included: Round-trip transportation from San José, Caribbean Coast, or La Fortuna
All safety equipment (life jacket, helmet, wetsuit if needed)
notIncluded: Hotels outside La Fortuna (extra transport fee)
